channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌏 Day 1 of Beijing Summit Produces No Big Wins for Trump | Foreign Policy
item.description: From the Iran war to trade, the U.S. president failed to secure major concessions from his counterpart.

item.title: 🌏 Trump, Xi Hold High-Stakes Summit in Beijing | Foreign Policy
item.description: From the Iran war to trade, the U.S. president failed to secure major concessions from his counterpart.

item.title: 🌍 CENTCOM Commander Dismisses Reports That Iran Retains Most Of Its Missile And Drone Arsenal | The War Zone
item.description: Admiral Brad Cooper said that "the numbers that I've seen in open source are not accurate" as reports mount that most of Iran's arsenal is intact. The post CENTCOM Commander Dismisses Reports That Iran Retains Most Of Its Missile And Drone Arsenal appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: ⚛️ The Pentagon wants 10,000 small cruise missiles. Here’s who is making them. | Breaking Defense
item.description: Editor-in-Chief Aaron Mehta explains the newest Pentagon munitions push.

item.title: 🏴 Zelenskyy raises alarm over Russia’s escalating ‘human safari’ in Ukraine | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has raised the alarm over Russia’s escalating “human safari” tactics targeting the civilian population in southern Ukraine’s Kherson region, writes Oleksandr Tolokonnikov. The post Zelenskyy raises alarm over Russia’s escalating ‘human…

item.title: 📍 To deliver for the warfighter, acquisition reform must provide a level playing field | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] When all companies in the market are allowed to compete under the same set of rules, innovation flourishes and our nation unleashes the full breadth of its technological strength.

item.title: 📍 Gerald R. Ford to return from historic deployment on Saturday: CNO | Breaking Defense
item.description: Aircraft from Carrier Air Wing 8 embarked on the Ford returned to their home naval air stations on Monday.

item.title: ⚛️ Nuclear-Powered Trump Class Battleships Will Reverse One Of The Navy’s “Largest Mistakes”: Navy Boss | The War Zone
item.description: The Chief of Naval Operations says giving up on nuclear-powered surface combatants was one of the worst decisions his service has ever made. The post Nuclear-Powered Trump Class Battleships Will Reverse One Of The Navy’s “Largest Mistakes”: Navy Boss appeared first on The War…

item.title: 🌍 Iran military threat is diminished but not eliminated, CENTCOM chief says | Defense News
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper said the U.S. had severely degraded Iran’s warfighting capacity, including the elimination of roughly 90% of its inventory of naval mines.

item.title: 🌍 Five ways the Iran war will forever alter the Middle East | Atlantic Council
item.description: Expect the war to weaken the Iranian regime, keep the US in the Middle East, harm Israel's standing in the region, and more. The post Five ways the Iran war will forever alter the Middle East appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Israel’s Lebanon Strategy Is Self-Defeating | Foreign Policy
item.description: A weakened state can’t disarm Hezbollah.

item.title: 🌍 In Talks with Israel, Lebanon is Walking a Thin Line | War on the Rocks
item.description: This week, Israel and Lebanon are holding their third round of unprecedented direct talks on crucial issues, including borders, the disarmament of Hizballah, and ongoing Israeli military action. The United States, which is clearly driving these talks, views them expansively as…

item.title: 🏴 Bulgaria is unlikely to become Putin’s new proxy within the European Union | Atlantic Council
item.description: Newly elected Bulgarian Prime Minister Rumen Radev is unlikely to replace recently ousted Hungarian leader Viktor Orban as Vladimir Putin's proxy within the EU and NATO, writes Kristian Kafozov. The post Bulgaria is unlikely to become Putin’s new proxy within the European Union…

item.title: 🌍 Iran’s Navy won’t rebuild for 5 to 10 years, country unable to support proxies: CENTCOM | Breaking Defense
item.description: “Today, Hamas, Hezbollah and the Houthis are all cut off from Iran’s weapons supply and support,” Adm. Brad Cooper, told SASC members.

item.title: 🌏 The Adversarial: Maximalist Demands, Victory Day, and a Summit | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Adversarial. Every other week, we’ll provide you with expert analysis on America’s greatest challengers: China, Russia, Iran, North Korea, and jihadists. Read more below.***IranA month after negotiators met for historic high-level talks in Pakistan, Washington and…

item.title: 🌏 Lipsky cited in Bloomberg on a potential rare earths and semiconductors deal between US and China | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read full article here The post Lipsky cited in Bloomberg on a potential rare earths and semiconductors deal between US and China appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 What Happened in India’s West Bengal? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The BJP decisively ousted longtime leaders in the state, but last-minute changes to the voter rolls have fueled allegations of foul play.

item.title: 📍 Defense Business Brief: Tulsa’s space draw; Cadenazzi’s wish; Anduril’s $5B round | Defense One
item.description: Click to read the full story at the source.

item.title: 🏛️ B-52 modernization ‘stabilized’ after cost spikes, schedule delays, Air Force official says | Breaking Defense
item.description: “It’s a lot of money. Some of us watch that money very carefully,” said Rep. Clay Higgins.

item.title: 📍 The Trump Administration’s Use of State Power Against Media: Keeping Track of the Big Picture | Just Security
item.description: Tracking the use of State power requires systematically identifying linkages between individual developments and broader trends. This interactive graphic offers one method. The post The Trump Administration’s Use of State Power Against Media: Keeping Track of the Big Picture…

item.title: 🌍 Israel’s F-35s Are Getting External Fuel Tanks | The War Zone
item.description: Fresh off extensive combat operations against Iran, Israel is boosting the F-35I Adir’s endurance with external fuel tanks that will also reduce dependence on tanker aircraft. The post Israel’s F-35s Are Getting External Fuel Tanks appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🛡️ CBO’s $1.2 trillion Golden Dome estimate based on bad data: Guetlein | Breaking Defense
item.description: Gen. Michael Guetlein, who previously said the project would cost $185 billion, insisted DoD would be able to deploy an “operational system” to defend the entire US homeland by 2028.

item.title: 🏛️ The case for a $24 Billion Special Operations budget | Breaking Defense
item.description: SOCOM’s budget has been flat since FY2019, but demand for SOF capabilities has risen 300 percent in the past five years, argue former acting SecDef Chris Miller and Phil Anderson of SOF Action.

item.title: 📍 This company wants to make 1,000 satellites a year | Defense One
item.description: Quantum Space is building a Tulsa factory for its not-yet-flown Ranger satellite.

item.title: 📍 Transnational Repression of Hong Kongers in the UK Is Disturbingly Common | The Diplomat
item.description: New survey data from Hong Kong Watch helps quantify the scale of the problem – including among Hong Kongers not engaged in public activism.

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏛️ USVs could be ‘alternate options’ for missions for stretched manned fleet: Navy official | Breaking Defense
item.description: USVs are emerging as modular force elements when sending a large combatant ship is too costly or disproportionate.

item.title: 🏴 Blacklists, corruption and frontline needs: Ukraine tackles an arms-export puzzle | Defense News
item.description: After years of struggling to arm its one million active-duty soldiers, Kyiv has been wary of allowing its domestic producers to sell their weapons abroad.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine UGV ‘Simba’ rolls out of the shadows in NATO Baltic exercise | Breaking Defense
item.description: In videos viewed by Breaking Defense, the Simba UGV is seen completing night missions even after losing a wheel and being struck by an FPV.

item.title: 🌏 Trump-Xi Summit Leaves South Korea Navigating Familiar Pressures | The Diplomat
item.description: For Seoul, what the leaders of the great superpowers left unsaid in Beijing may matter more than what they agreed on.
